output: Final verification reran successfully where the repo allowed it. `cd banodoco-website && npm test -- --run` passed (`17` files, `100` tests). The throwaway `/tmp/repro_discord_sync.sh` repro was created, run, and deleted; after fixing the promoter's invalid `ON CONFLICT (...)` use against partial unique indexes, it printed `PASS` and confirmed one imported asset with non-null `discord_guild_id`, one imported comment with non-null `discord_guild_id`, two `media_import_jobs`, and zero new rows on rerun. task_updates.executor_notes: `supabase start` reached migration execution, but `supabase db reset --no-seed --yes` still fails at pre-existing migration `20260320000000_multi_server.sql` with `ERROR: column c.speaker_mode does not exist`, so the repo cannot be reset end-to-end yet. `supabase gen types ...` succeeded to `/tmp/banodoco_types_t21.ts`; the output still does not contain `asset_comments` or `media_import_jobs`, consistent with the linked project not having these migrations deployed. `cd banodoco-website && npm test -- --run` passes (`17` files, `100` tests). `pytest --tb=no -q --no-header` still reports the same 7 recurring repo-wide failures and no new failures. The throwaway `/tmp/repro_discord_sync.sh` script was created, run, and deleted. Its first run exposed a real bug in `supabase/migrations/20260423000005_discord_promoter_fn.sql`: `internal.discord_promote_resources()` used `ON CONFLICT (discord_thread_id)` / `ON CONFLICT (discord_attachment_id)` against partial unique indexes, which PostgreSQL cannot infer. I patched those inserts to use bare `ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING`, reran the script, and it printed `PASS`, confirming one imported asset row with non-null `discord_guild_id`, one imported comment row with non-null `discord_guild_id`, two `media_import_jobs`, and zero net new rows on the rerun idempotency gate. No browser automation tooling is available here, so the human-verification criteria were not executed beyond component-level smoke checks.

The website suite did pass, the throwaway Discord-sync repro script passed after fixing the promoter's invalid partial-index conflict targets, the rerun produced zero new rows, the script was deleted, and browser automation is unavailable so human-verification fell back to component/test smoke checks.
